Title: Convergent evolution of a blood-red nectar pigment in vertebrate-pollinated flowers
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: secreted extracellular nectar protein; multimeric catalase in nectar
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: desiccation-related protein with ferritin-like domain; ferritin-like Mn catalase-like
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: boiling abolished catalase activity; exogenous H2O2 degraded nesocodin unless protected by nectar proteins or catalase
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Nectary RNA-seq: NCBI SRA GSE149898; nectar proteomics: PRIDE PXD029394
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: nectar proteomics; in-gel catalase assay; spectrophotometric catalase assay; pigment protection assays with natural and synthetic nesocodin
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NmNec2 is a ferritin-like catalase present in nectar as a multimeric protein. 2. NmNec2 removes H2O2, a by-product of NmNec3 oxidase activity. 3. Catalase activity protects nesocodin from oxidative degradation and increases final pigment yield.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stabilizes and increases nesocodin accumulation by protecting the pigment from H2O2-mediated degradation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important accessory gene for metabolic engineering of oxidase-driven extracellular pigments because it prevents peroxide-mediated loss of product.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of catalase activity would increase oxidative degradation of pigment; broader physiological tradeoffs not tested.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it is a protective/stability factor rather than direct biosynthetic catalyst or regulator.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes reversible hydration of CO2 to bicarbonate and protons; in nectar context promotes alkalinization via bicarbonate-dependent pH increase.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for nectar pH and nesocodin formation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: nectar maturation/flower age from 0 h to +24 h; carbonic anhydrase inhibitors sulfanilamide, acetazolamide, and 6-ethoxy-2-benzothiazolesulfonamide
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Nectary RNA-seq: NCBI SRA GSE149898; nectar proteomics: PRIDE PXD029394
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: nectar proteomics; translated nectary transcriptome identification; in-gel carbonic anhydrase assay; inhibitor assays in vitro and in situ; pH and absorbance measurements
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NmNec1 was identified as a secreted α-carbonic anhydrase present in nectar. 2. NmNec1 activity increased nectar pH, and pharmacological inhibition reduced alkalinization and red pigment accumulation. 3. Nectar alkalinization driven by NmNec1 was necessary for efficient nesocodin formation and red coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nectar alkalinization and thereby enables red nesocodin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates that engineering extracellular pH control can enable or enhance formation of pH-dependent floral pigments in nectar-like secretions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in nectar chemical environment rather than directly synthesizing nesocodin; all three nectar proteins carry predicted N-terminal signal peptides.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GMC flavoenzyme oxidoreductase; mandelonitrile lyase-like protein
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes sinapyl alcohol to sinapaldehyde, generating hydrogen peroxide; sinapaldehyde then condenses with proline to form nesocodin.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point; necessary precursor-forming step
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: alkaline nectar conditions (50 mM Na(H)CO3, pH 9.0) support sinapaldehyde and nesocodin formation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Nectary RNA-seq: NCBI SRA GSE149898; nectar proteomics: PRIDE PXD029394
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: nectar proteomics; purified protein assay; flavoprotein verification; alcohol oxidase assays; metabolite profiling; in vitro reconstitution with proline to generate nesocodin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NmNec3 is a secreted GMC oxidoreductase with alcohol oxidase activity. 2. Purified NmNec3 converted sinapyl alcohol into sinapaldehyde, the immediate precursor of nesocodin. 3. In the presence of proline, NmNec3-supported reactions produced red nesocodin-like pigment.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nesocodin accumulation by supplying sinapaldehyde precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing nectar pigment biosynthesis when combined with alkalinization and peroxide-detoxifying modules.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Produces H2O2 as by-product, which can degrade pigment unless catalase activity is also present.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The final condensation of sinapaldehyde with proline appears nonenzymatic in vivo; NmNec3 belongs to a different oxidase family than Jaltomata JhNec7, supporting convergent evolution.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes reversible hydration of CO2; associated with alkaline nectar permissive for nesocodin accumulation in J. herrerae.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for alkaline nectar-associated pigment formation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: comparison of alkaline red nectar in J. herrerae versus acidic colorless nectar in J. procumbens
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Nectary RNA-seq: NCBI SRA GSE191201; nectar proteomics: PRIDE PXD029395
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: nectar proteomics; in-gel carbonic anhydrase activity assay; comparative nectar chemistry
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. J. herrerae nectar contains an α-carbonic anhydrase, JhNec5. 2. JhNec5 is associated with highly alkaline nectar that contains nesocodin. 3. The related species J. procumbens lacks this carbonic anhydrase activity and has acidic colorless nectar, supporting a role in pigment-permissive alkalinization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with nectar alkalinization and nesocodin-containing red nectar in J. herrerae.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights independent recruitment of extracellular carbonic anhydrase activity as a route to evolve colored nectar.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shares only ~42% identity with NmNec1 and is not its ortholog, supporting convergent evolution.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: berberine-bridge enzyme family; FAD/FMN-containing dehydrogenase superfamily
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes sinapyl alcohol to sinapaldehyde, supplying the aldehyde precursor required for nesocodin formation in alkaline nectar.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: comparison with J. procumbens nectar and enzyme activity assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Nectary RNA-seq: NCBI SRA GSE191201; nectar proteomics: PRIDE PXD029395
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: nectar proteomics; purified enzyme assay; raw nectar activity assay; comparative species analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. J. herrerae nectar contains a sinapyl alcohol oxidase, JhNec7. 2. JhNec7 converts sinapyl alcohol to sinapaldehyde, analogous to NmNec3 function in Nesocodon. 3. JhNec7 belongs to a different enzyme family from NmNec3, supporting convergent evolution of the same biochemical function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nesocodin accumulation by supplying sinapaldehyde precursor in J. herrerae nectar.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Illustrates that distinct oxidase scaffolds can be recruited to generate the same nectar pigment precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely generates H2O2 as oxidase by-product; specific protective partner not identified in J. herrerae in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not orthologous to NmNec3 and shares only ~21% identity; evidence favors convergent evolution.
